tech_stack: Stitch MCP (@_davideast/stitch-mcp proxy) as primary design engine
W3C DTCG .tokens.json (2025.10) for design token export
Node.js HTTP server for local preview
existing_assets: commands/discover.md — current discover prompt with Step 0c at lines 41-55
schemas/discover.schema.json — v4.0 schema with 6-field constraints object
Stitch MCP configured and verified (API key, proxy mode, 12 tools available)
Interactive preview POC: device simulation + tab switching + iframe rendering
Stitch variant generation POC: generate_screen_from_text + generate_variants verified

additional.stitch_dependency_strategy: Stitch preferred, Tier 2 (agent HTML) must also achieve relatively high quality
additional.documentation: README must be updated with Stitch setup guide and usage tutorial after integration

selected_direction.description: Open Design Co-pilot — Platform-agnostic interactive design preview engine with adapter architecture (Stitch primary, agent HTML fallback), providing full interactive design co-creation experience in the discover phase: multi-variant side-by-side comparison, device simulation, text micro-feedback, hybrid DNA selection, iteration history rollback.
selected_direction.differentiator: Only tool in the market that embeds interactive UI design exploration in the requirements discovery phase. Not locked to any single design vendor. CLI-native with zero context-switching.
selected_direction.rationale: Merged Direction 2 (open adapters) + Direction 4 (design co-pilot) to maximize both extensibility and user experience completeness. User explicitly prioritized experience completeness over delivery speed (12/12 features kept, zero cuts).

pre_mortem.scenario: Stitch API quota exhausted mid-session, user stuck waiting
pre_mortem.cause: 10 pages x 5 variants x 2-3 iteration rounds = ~150 API calls exceeds free tier
pre_mortem.early_warning: Track API call count from first generation; warn at 80% estimated quota

pre_mortem.scenario: Tier 2 agent HTML quality perceived as unusably low compared to Stitch
pre_mortem.cause: LLM-generated HTML lacks the design polish of Gemini 3.1 Pro + Stitch rendering pipeline
pre_mortem.early_warning: A/B test Tier 1 vs Tier 2 output on 5 reference designs during development

pre_mortem.scenario: Adapter abstraction over-engineered, delays delivery with no near-term payoff
pre_mortem.cause: Only 2 providers initially; generic interface adds complexity without immediate multi-source usage
pre_mortem.early_warning: If DesignProvider interface definition exceeds 1 day, simplify

pre_mortem.scenario: designMd to DTCG token conversion produces inaccurate or incomplete tokens
pre_mortem.cause: Stitch Markdown format is unstructured; LLM extraction misses or misinterprets values
pre_mortem.early_warning: First batch conversion results: human audit pass rate < 80% triggers redesign

pre_mortem.scenario: Selection callback fails in enterprise/SSH/WSL/Docker environments
pre_mortem.cause: Localhost POST blocked by firewall, network namespace isolation, or proxy settings
pre_mortem.early_warning: Test in 3+ distinct environments before shipping

design_philosophy.justification: 12 features全Keep，拒绝轻量方案。产品完整度是不可妥协的底线。
即使Stitch是当前最佳选择，仍要求适配器抽象 + DTCG标准格式，为Figma/v0扩展留口。
Stitch不可用时不回退到残废体验，Tier 2必须'较高质量'。每一层降级都有质量下限。
选择自动回传、端口自动检测、环境自动适配、临时文件自动清理——所有机械步骤系统化。
配额耗尽要通知、生成过程要进度提示、降级切换要告知。系统状态不隐藏。

stack: Stitch MCP (@_davideast/stitch-mcp proxy) — primary design provider
Claude agent HTML generation — Tier 2 fallback provider
Node.js http module — local preview server
W3C DTCG .tokens.json — design token interchange format
tech_direction.architecture_style: Adapter pattern (DesignProvider interface) + local HTTP server for preview + prompt-based orchestration (commands/discover.md)
tech_direction.product_impact: Pluggable design sources mean users are never locked to one vendor. Local preview server means zero cloud dependency for the selection UX. DTCG tokens mean build-phase tools can consume design decisions without manual translation.
tech_direction.rationale: Leverages existing NoPilot TypeScript infrastructure. Stitch provides Gemini 3.1 Pro design quality without building a design engine. DTCG is the 2025.10 industry standard with Style Dictionary ecosystem for multi-platform output.

entities.name: DesignProvider
entities.description: Abstract interface for design generation sources (Stitch, AgentHTML, future Figma/v0)

entities.name: DesignVariant
entities.description: A single UI style variant containing HTML code, screenshot, and metadata

entities.name: PreviewSession
entities.description: A browser-based interactive preview session with variant collection, device config, and user selection state

entities.name: DesignDNA
entities.description: Extracted style genome from variants: color palette, typography scale, spacing, border-radius, animation level

entities.name: DesignToken
entities.description: W3C DTCG formatted design token exported from DesignDNA for build-phase consumption

entities.name: SelectionResult
entities.description: User's selection outcome including chosen variant ID, text feedback, hybrid DNA annotations, iteration history

entities.name: PageSpec
entities.description: Specification for a page needing design: name, target platform, device type, existing style constraints

relationships.description: Provider generates multiple variants per page
Session presents all variants for one page
Selected variant's style genome is extracted
DNA is exported as DTCG tokens
DNA constrains next page's variant generation for cross-page consistency

non_functional_requirements.description: End-to-end time from variant generation start to preview visible
Preview page load time in browser
Automatic degradation when Stitch is unavailable
non_functional_requirements.target: Degradation switch < 5 seconds, no user intervention required

non_functional_requirements.description: Stitch API key must not enter git repository
non_functional_requirements.target: Key only in ~/.mcp.json or environment variables

non_functional_requirements.description: Support SSH remote and local environments
non_functional_requirements.target: Auto-detect and adapt (URL display vs browser auto-open)
Chrome, Firefox, Safari all render correctly

non_functional_requirements.description: Maximum pages per single discover session

mvp_features.name: Design source adapter architecture
mvp_features.competitive_notes: No competitor has pluggable design source architecture in CLI workflows

mvp_features.name: Multi-variant generation
mvp_features.competitive_notes: v0 generates single variant; Stitch generates 2-4; we generate 5+ with creative range control

mvp_features.name: Interactive preview engine
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Market blank — no competitor offers device-simulated multi-variant preview in CLI workflow

mvp_features.name: Selection callback closed loop
mvp_features.competitive_notes: All competitors require manual context switching

mvp_features.name: Iterative refinement engine
mvp_features.competitive_notes: No competitor supports hybrid DNA selection or iteration history in design exploration

mvp_features.name: Existing project style detection
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Stitch has experimental URL extraction; we do source-code-level analysis

mvp_features.name: Cross-page style consistency
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Figma does this natively; we bring it to CLI-native workflow

mvp_features.name: Design token export (DTCG)
mvp_features.competitive_notes: No tool exports DTCG tokens from requirements discovery phase

mvp_features.name: Dark/Light mode pairing
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Stitch supports colorMode; we integrate into selection flow

context_dependencies: Google Stitch MCP availability and API stability
Stitch API free tier quota limits
Browser support for backdrop-filter and modern CSS features in preview rendering
